This value is equal to '(2 ** 59) * log(p)' where 'p' is the intended price limit and 'log' denotes the natural logarithm operator.","poolId":"The target pool identifier.","zeroForOne":"If \u2018zeroForOne & ((1 << 128) - 1) == 0' then tag0 is taken from and tag1 is given to the pool. If  \u2018zeroForOne & ((1 << 128) - 1) == 1' then tag1 is taken from and tag0 is given to the pool. If \u2018zeroForOne & ((1 << 128) - 1)\u2019 is greater than 1, then the direction of the swap is towards 'logPriceLimit'. \u2018(zeroForOne >> 128)\u2019 is a threshold on the number of shares that should be available in any interval for the swap function to transact in that  interval. For example, if (zeroForOne >> 128 == 0), which is the default, no minimum number of shares is imposed."},"returns":{"amount0":"The amount of tag0 swapped. updated."},"OperatorSet(address,address,bool)":{"notice":"Emitted when an operator is assigned/absolved by the owner.See ERC6909 specifications."},"PoolCollection(uint256,address,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when the pool owner's accrued growth portions are collected."},"ProtocolCollection(uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when the protocol owner's accrued growth portions are collected."},"Swap(uint256,address,bytes32)":{"notice":"Emitted when a swap is performed. The last two members of the curve are logged which are sufficient to uniquely characterize the state. In other words, if we know both 'qTarget' and 'qOvershoot' that appear at the end of the amended curve, then we can deterministically track the state of each pool without having to perform any numerical search."},"Transfer(address,address,address,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when a tag is transferred.See ERC6909 specifications."},"UpdateGrowthPortions(uint256,address,bytes32)":{"notice":"Emitted when the growth portions for a nofeeswap pool are synced with the protocol or sentinel contract."}},"kind":"user","methods":{"allowance(address,address,uint256)":{"notice":"See ERC6909 specifications."},"approve(address,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"See ERC6909 specifications."},"balanceOf(address,uint256)":{"notice":"See ERC6909 specifications."},"clear(uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"WARNING - Once a balance is cleared, the corresponding funds become permanently inaccessible and remain locked within the contract. Executing a clear call will erase the entire balance owed to the caller without initiating any outbound transfer.Clears transient balance owed to 'msg.sender'."},"dispatch(bytes)":{"notice":"Gives access to 'NofeeswapDelegatee.sol' functionalities."},"isOperator(address,address)":{"notice":"See ERC6909 specifications."},"modifyBalance(address,uint256,int256)":{"notice":"Mints/burns singleton balance. This results in a transient storage balance which must be settled. transient balance <-> singleton balance"},"modifyBalance(address,uint256,uint256,int256,int256)":{"notice":"Mints/burns singleton double balance of tags 0 and 1. Can be used for gas efficient transactions by eliminating the need to update two  storage slots. This results in transient storage balances which must be  settled. transient balance of tags 0 and 1 <-> singleton double balance"},"onERC1155BatchReceived(address,address,uint256[],uint256[],bytes)":{"notice":"See ERC1155TokenReceiver specifications."},"onERC1155Received(address,address,uint256,uint256,bytes)":{"notice":"See ERC1155TokenReceiver specifications."},"setOperator(address,bool)":{"notice":"See ERC6909 specifications."},"settle()":{"notice":"Should be called after ERC-20, ERC6909, or ERC1155 transfers to the protocol to update transient balances. Can be used to send native tokens to the protocol to settle native transient balance of  'msg.sender'."},"storageAccess(bytes32)":{"notice":"Provides access to a single Nofeeswap storage slot."},"storageAccess(bytes32,uint256)":{"notice":"Provides access to consecutive Nofeeswap storage slots."},"storageAccess(bytes32[])":{"notice":"Provides access to a given list of Nofeeswap storage slots."},"supportsInterface(bytes4)":{"notice":"See ERC6909 specifications."},"swap(uint256,int256,int256,uint256,bytes)":{"notice":"Performs a swap."},"sync(address)":{"notice":"Synchronizes the protocol ERC-20 balance. Should be called prior to ERC-20 transfers to the protocol."},"sync(address,uint256)":{"notice":"Synchronizes the protocol ERC-1155 or ERC-6909 balance. Should be  called prior to ERC-1155 or ERC-6909 transfers to the protocol."},"take(address,address,uint256)":{"notice":"Pays native or ERC-20 assets from the protocol to 'to'. This results in a transient storage balance which must be settled. transient balance -> 'to' wallet"},"take(address,uint256,address,uint256)":{"notice":"Pays ERC-6909 assets from the protocol to 'to'. This results in a transient storage balance which must be settled. transient balance -> 'to' wallet"},"take(address,uint256,address,uint256,bytes)":{"notice":"Pays ERC-1155 assets from the protocol to 'to'. params.amount: The amount to be cleared which must be equal to the current transient balance of the caller.
params.tag: The tag whose transient balance to be cleared.

params.input: Encoded input via 'abi.encodeWithSelector' to be passed as  delegate call to 'NofeeswapDelegatee.sol'.

params.amount: The amount to be added (positive) or removed (negative) to/from the owner's singleton balance.
params.tag: The tag whose balance to be modified.

params.amount0: The amount of tag0 to be added (positive) or removed  (negative) to/from the owner's singleton balance.
params.amount1: The amount of tag1 to be added (positive) or removed  (negative) to/from the owner's singleton balance.

returns._0: The value of the slot as bytes32

params.nSlots: Number of slots to be read.

params.slots: List of slots to sload from.

params.amountSpecified: The amount to be given to (positive) or taken  from (negative) the pool.
params.hookData: The data to be passed to the hook.
params.logPriceLimit: The limit logPrice constraint. This value is equal to '(2 ** 59) * log(p)' where 'p' is the intended price limit and 'log' denotes the natural logarithm operator.
params.zeroForOne: If ‘zeroForOne & ((1 << 128) - 1) == 0' then tag0 is taken from and tag1 is given to the pool. If  ‘zeroForOne & ((1 << 128) - 1) == 1' then tag1 is taken from and tag0 is given to the pool. If ‘zeroForOne & ((1 << 128) - 1)’ is greater than 1, then the direction of the swap is towards 'logPriceLimit'. ‘(zeroForOne >> 128)’ is a threshold on the number of shares that should be available in any interval for the swap function to transact in that  interval. For example, if (zeroForOne >> 128 == 0), which is the default, no minimum number of shares is imposed.

returns.amount0: The amount of tag0 swapped. Positive values are incoming to the pool and negative values are outgoing from the pool.
returns.amount1: The amount of tag1 swapped. Positive values are incoming to the pool and negative values are outgoing from the pool.

params.token: ERC-1155 or ERC-6909 address to be synced.

params.amount: The amount of tokens to be taken from the protocol.
params.token: ERC-20 or native tokens to be taken from the protocol's wallet.

params.amount: The amount of tokens to be taken from the protocol.
params.tokenId: Multi-token id to be taken from the protocol's wallet.

params.amount: The amount of tokens to be taken from the protocol.
params.tokenId: Multi-token id to be taken from the protocol's wallet.

params.tag: The tag whose balance to be transferred.

returns._0: The value of the slot as bytes32

params.nSlots: Number of slots to be read.

params.slots: List of slots to tload from.

params.data: The data/instructions to be passed to the target contract.
params.unlockTarget: The target contract address which must implement  'IUnlockCallback.sol'.

returns.result: The output from the target contract.

clear(uint256,uint256).notice: WARNING - Once a balance is cleared, the corresponding funds become permanently inaccessible and remain locked within the contract. Executing a clear call will erase the entire balance owed to the caller without initiating any outbound transfer.Clears transient balance owed to 'msg.sender'.

modifyBalance(address,uint256,int256).notice: Mints/burns singleton balance. This results in a transient storage balance which must be settled. transient balance <-> singleton balance

modifyBalance(address,uint256,uint256,in.notice: Mints/burns singleton double balance of tags 0 and 1. Can be used for gas efficient transactions by eliminating the need to update two  storage slots. This results in transient storage balances which must be  settled. transient balance of tags 0 and 1 <-> singleton double balance

settle().notice: Should be called after ERC-20, ERC6909, or ERC1155 transfers to the protocol to update transient balances. Can be used to send native tokens to the protocol to settle native transient balance of  'msg.sender'.

storageAccess(bytes32).notice: Provides access to a single Nofeeswap storage slot.

storageAccess(bytes32,uint256).notice: Provides access to consecutive Nofeeswap storage slots.

storageAccess(bytes32[]).notice: Provides access to a given list of Nofeeswap storage slots.

sync(address).notice: Synchronizes the protocol ERC-20 balance. Should be called prior to ERC-20 transfers to the protocol.

sync(address,uint256).notice: Synchronizes the protocol ERC-1155 or ERC-6909 balance. Should be  called prior to ERC-1155 or ERC-6909 transfers to the protocol.

take(address,address,uint256).notice: Pays native or ERC-20 assets from the protocol to 'to'. This results in a transient storage balance which must be settled. transient balance -> 'to' wallet

take(address,uint256,address,uint256).notice: Pays ERC-6909 assets from the protocol to 'to'. This results in a transient storage balance which must be settled. transient balance -> 'to' wallet

take(address,uint256,address,uint256,byt.notice: Pays ERC-1155 assets from the protocol to 'to'. This results in a transient storage balance which must be settled. transient balance -> 'to' wallet

transferTransientBalanceFrom(address,add.notice: Transfers transient balance from 'sender' to 'receiver'. Can be used to settle on behalf of other accounts.

transientAccess(bytes32).notice: Provides access to a single Nofeeswap transient storage slot.

transientAccess(bytes32,uint256).notice: Provides access to consecutive Nofeeswap transient storage slots.

transientAccess(bytes32[]).notice: Provides access to a given list of Nofeeswap transient storage slots.

unlock(address,bytes).notice: This function gives access to the following protocol methods that are safeguarded against reentrancy:   'INofeeswap.clear(Tag,uint256)'   'INofeeswap.take(address,address,uint256)'   'INofeeswap.take(address,uint256,address,uint256)'   'INofeeswap.take(address,uint256,address,uint256,bytes)'   'INofeeswap.settle(address)'   'INofeeswap.transferTransientBalanceFrom(address,address,Tag,uint256)'   'INofeeswap.modifyBalance(address,Tag,int256)'   'INofeeswap.modifyBalance(address,Tag,Tag,int256,int256)'   'INofeeswap.swap(uint256,int256,X59,uint256,bytes)'   'INofeeswapDelegatee.modifyPosition(uint256,X59,X59,int256,bytes)'   'INofeeswapDelegatee.donate(uint256,uint256,bytes)'
